why_it_matters: The iOS distribution needs a published 1.1.0 runtime so SwiftPM consumers can adopt shared playback headers with the same semantics already verified in LegatoCore tests.
user_impact: iOS hosts can reuse immutable shared header groups, preserve track-level override precedence, and keep effective headers internal while supporting mixed-token playlists.
upgrade_notes: Upgrade the iOS distribution to LegatoCore v1.1.0 and align the Legato package line to 1.1.0 before using shared playback headers in host apps.
highlights: LegatoCore 1.1.0 publishes shared playback header-group resolution for iOS.
Unknown `headerGroupId` values fail fast before queue mutation.
Public snapshots/events keep declarative track shape without leaking secrets.
known_limitations: Shared header groups are static setup-time declarations; token refresh and DRM remain out of scope.
This release does not claim full lifecycle/background production hardening beyond the already documented scope.
